best_simplified.label: Source-bound score candidate with review gate

lowest_false_positive_simplified.label: Source-bound score candidate with review gate

mutants.description: Policy treats authority and counter-material coverage as screening status.
Policy treats decision support as complete without adoption reasons or contestation logging.
Policy no longer requires a source-linked evidence packet.
Policy stops requiring high-authority material coverage.
Policy accepts nonprocedural source tags as if they were verified.
Policy allows high status without a review and contestability gate.
Policy treats recall at or above 0.8 as screening status.
Policy treats provider, model or engine identity as screening status.
Policy treats review posture and role readiness as screening status.
Policy treats source-bound evidence as screening status.
Policy treats total score at or above 9 as screening status.

substitute_theory_falsification.additional_evidence: 153/215 high-recall outputs were blocked below normative screening
Source links without authority, counter-material and contestability gates over-admit outputs
substitute_theory_falsification.substitute: Source-bound evidence alone confers procedural status

substitute_theory_falsification.additional_evidence: Authority and counter-material coverage without source-chain, role and contestability gates over-admit outputs
substitute_theory_falsification.substitute: High-authority and counter-material coverage confer procedural status

substitute_theory_falsification.additional_evidence: Review labels without source-chain and failure-cap gates over-admit outputs
substitute_theory_falsification.substitute: Review posture or role readiness confers procedural status

substitute_theory_falsification.additional_evidence: High total score cannot substitute for missing legal-material predicates
substitute_theory_falsification.substitute: Total audit score confers procedural status

substitute_theory_falsification.additional_evidence: 1005/1320 identity-labelled rows would be over-admitted; identity substitution produced 0 status changes
substitute_theory_falsification.substitute: Provider, model or claimed-capability labels confer procedural status

suites.finding: Tests downgrade, withdrawal, decision-support and high-recall-but-blocked behavior.
suites.label: Protocol stress scenarios

suites.finding: Tests source reconstruction across six public legal-record sources.
suites.label: Public legal-record metadata

suites.finding: Tests ordered real upstream legal-output reconstruction.
suites.label: Public legal-system outputs

suites.finding: Tests public issue-search outputs and a source-bound public-source packet against high-authority and counter-material requirements.
suites.label: Issue-specific public output/source packets

suites.finding: Tests public case-law or known-item outputs against authority sets that the endpoint is capable of returning, while recording mixed-authority gaps separately.
suites.label: Endpoint-matched public retrieval benchmark

suites.finding: Tests withheld public-retrieval packets, raw model-output packets and source-bound repair packets after freezing the scoring policy.
suites.label: Out-of-sample holdout validation

suites.finding: Tests whether strong authority coverage without source binding remains procedurally capped.
suites.label: Raw Codex GPT-5.5 xhigh outputs

suites.finding: Tests whether model-output variants can qualify after manifest, locator, issue-set, rank-salience and hashed source-support evidence validation.
suites.label: Source-supported model-output repairs

suites.finding: Tests whether high-recall outputs from three model engines remain capped without source-bound legal-material chains.
suites.label: Cross-engine raw model outputs

suites.finding: Tests whether the same issue outputs qualify after source-support validation, regardless of model identity.
suites.label: Cross-engine source-supported repairs

suites.finding: Tests model-output variants across raw, source-bound, counter-material, rank-salience, contestability, logging, authorized-adoption and unauthorized-action conditions.
suites.label: Model-output evidence ladder

suites.finding: Tests whether source-support repair gates reject locator mismatches, unsupported claims, contradiction patterns, out-of-manifest sources, missing output links and counter-material omissions.
suites.label: Adversarial source-support repairs

suites.finding: Tests normative material screening with mixed statute/case/source packets rather than single-endpoint public search results.
suites.label: Mixed-authority public source-screening packets

suites.finding: Tests whether high-authority omissions, counter-material suppression, unverified source tags and missing adoption gates trigger the expected caps.
suites.label: Issue-defined ablations

suites.finding: Applies every nonempty combination of locator, output-source link, procedural source tag, high-authority recall and counter-material recall attacks across every qualified packet; all attacked variants must lose high status through downgrade, suspension or withdrawal despite high scores and high upstream recall.
suites.label: Qualified-output source-chain attacks

suites.finding: Applies valid counter-material, source-verification, jurisdiction and contestability-channel challenges plus unsupported challenge controls across every qualified packet; valid challenges must block high status while unsupported challenges preserve it.
suites.label: Dynamic contestation challenges

suites.finding: Checks issue-manifest support terms against extracted public source text snapshots to reduce manifest-only source-support circularity.
suites.label: Public source-text anchors

suites.finding: Checks that raw model-output scenario locators are anchored in the committed transcript sections.
suites.label: Model-output transcript anchors

suites.finding: Checks that cross-engine raw-output locators are anchored in committed transcripts across three model engines.
suites.label: Cross-engine transcript anchors

suites.finding: Exhaustively tests monotonicity, evidence-packet necessity, authority-set necessity, counter-material necessity, adoption necessity, role caps, failure caps and metric non-equivalence.
suites.label: Formal invariant verification

suites.finding: Exhausts the high-status claim-attempt lattice and shows that score, role, source and authority substitutes over-admit unless the full screening predicate is present.
suites.label: Status-lattice exhaustion
suites.validation_units: 233280 high-status claim-attempt states, 1632960 cover edges and 1632960 substitute-rule predictions

suites.finding: Quantifies that upstream precision, recall and F1 are weak predictors of procedural qualification, while audit gates remove high-recall false positives.
suites.label: Metric separation analysis

suites.finding: Compares recall, F1, total-score, source-bound, authority-material and review-gate substitutes against the protocol-defined reference allocation, showing that every simplified rule either over-admits or misses procedurally qualified packets.
suites.label: Baseline rule comparison
suites.rule_pass: best simplified FP 38; reference rule FP 0

suites.finding: Removes evidence, source-tag, authority, counter-material, review, role-cap and adoption gates from qualified packets and verifies that status falls below the corresponding procedural level.
suites.label: Qualified-output gate ablations

suites.finding: Preserves each qualified packet's audit score vector, upstream metrics and system role while flipping one mandatory gate; every witness pair separates allowed procedural status, proving score-only and retrieval-metric-only substitutes cannot reproduce the protocol on the validation domain.
suites.label: Gate-contrast witness pairs

suites.finding: Computes the minimal artefact-gate families needed to upgrade each blocked normative-screening or decision-support claim.
suites.label: Blocked-claim repair frontiers

suites.finding: Tests that high-status outputs preserve status under valid generic profile assumptions but downgrade when jurisdiction assumptions are absent or profile-mismatched.
suites.label: Jurisdiction-profile mutations

suites.finding: Computes a rank-window visibility curve for counter-material salience and applies rank-order counterfactuals where the packet contains enough non-counter material to move counter-material below the visibility window.
suites.label: Ranking-visibility diagnostics
suites.validation_units: 956 rank-window checks over 248 high-status claims plus 85 rank-order counterfactuals

suites.finding: Generates proof-carrying status certificates for every scenario and replays scenario identity, scenario hash, policy hash, policy body, jurisdiction profile, score gate, role cap, missing gates, failure cap, metric bundle, claim support, proof obligations and derivation hash.
suites.label: Proof-carrying status certificates
suites.validation_units: 6072 replay checks and 4488 proof obligations over 264 certificates

suites.finding: Mutates certificate identities, hashes, policy bodies, scores, roles, gates, status fields, caps, metric bundles, proof obligations and certificate-set structure; every tampered proof object must be rejected.
suites.label: Certificate tamper-resistance

suites.finding: Recomputes score candidates, role caps, protected reliance gates, missing gates, failure caps, metrics and final status in a separate script parameterized by JSON policy constants without importing the harness model.
suites.label: Policy-constants replay

suites.finding: Applies claim escalation, metric inflation, role-cap demotion, source-tag mutation, review-gate removal, score-and-role inflation without adoption, and benign-source augmentation to primary scenario packets without using expected labels.
suites.label: Metamorphic policy tests

suites.finding: Applies gate-removal and status-conferring policy mutants across committed packets; every mutant is killed because removing a required gate, ignoring a cap, or treating metrics, source labels, review labels, total score or model identity as status produces a wrong allocation.
suites.label: Policy mutation analysis

suites.finding: Substitutes frontier, legal-specialist, open-weight, small-model and undisclosed-agentic identity labels across every packet; procedural status remains unchanged because model identity is not a status-conferring property.
suites.label: Model-identity invariance
suites.validation_units: 1320 identity mutations over 264 packets and 5 model identities

suites.finding: Compares issue-equivalent public-search query variants and holdout variants, showing whether authority coverage, counter-material recall, top-result identity and retrieved-record sets remain stable under query reformulation.
suites.label: Query-perturbation stability

suites.finding: Enumerates all non-empty query portfolios over issue-equivalent public retrieval variants to test whether frozen query expansion recovers high-authority and counter-material coverage.
suites.label: Query-portfolio frontier
suites.validation_units: 315 query portfolios plus 5 group frontier summaries

suites.finding: Tests whether status allocation survives strict and lenient recoding of the same evidence packets.
suites.label: Annotation robustness recoding

suites.finding: Perturbs all six audit scores under a fixed-seed Monte Carlo model to locate boundary cases and test whether status allocation is robust to plausible coding noise.
suites.label: Annotation uncertainty Monte Carlo

suites.finding: Tests score-blinded coder reliability, base-status tracking and dimension-level score calibration under class imbalance.
suites.label: Score-blinded dual coding
suites.rule_pass: 0.99 coder kappa; 0.37 weakest base-dimension kappa (Q); 0.87 min base-dimension exact; 0.13 max score drift
